best_qa_per_chain.question: Elizabeth of York biography facts
best_qa_per_chain.answer: Elizabeth of York was the daughter of Edward IV and Elizabeth Woodville, and she became the queen consort of Henry VII of England. Her marriage to Henry VII united the warring houses of York and Lancaster, solidifying his claim to the throne. Elizabeth was the mother of several children, including Henry VIII, and played a significant role in the Tudor dynasty's establishment.

citations.title: Biography of Elizabeth of York, Queen of England
Reading History: “Elizabeth of York: A Tudor Queen and Her World
Elizabeth of York: A Tudor Queen and Her World by Alison Weir

best_qa_per_chain.question: Elizabeth of York marriage to Henry VII
best_qa_per_chain.answer: Elizabeth of York married Henry VII in 1486, uniting the rival houses of Lancaster and York after the Wars of the Roses. Their marriage was both a political alliance and a personal relationship marked by affection, contributing to the stability of Henry's reign. Elizabeth was the daughter of Edward IV, and their union helped solidify Henry's claim to the throne.

headline: this accessible bathroom has an angled mirror for wheelchair users and the emergency pull cord lines the bottom of the walls in case someones stuck on the floor

best_qa_per_chain.question: accessible bathroom design features wheelchair users
best_qa_per_chain.answer: Accessible bathroom design features for wheelchair users include sufficient floor space for maneuverability (typically 60 inches for a 180-degree turn), accessible doors, grab bars, comfort-height toilets, and safety features like shower seats. Additionally, sinks should be at an appropriate height and allow for wheelchair access underneath.

citations.title: How To Design an Accessible Bathroom
Handicap Bathroom Remodeling & Age in Place

best_qa_per_chain.answer: Angled mirrors can enhance the functionality of wheelchair accessible bathrooms by improving visibility and light distribution. While specific guidelines for mirror placement in accessible bathrooms are not detailed in the provided sources, general accessibility principles suggest that mirrors should be positioned to accommodate users in wheelchairs. This typically means ensuring that the bottom edge of the mirror is no higher than 40 inches from the floor.
